app.description: Detect and repair DNS / connectivity issues on Windows, tuned for China + soft-router-via-Japan setups.
cmd.recommend.help: Speed-test + pollution-check public DNS, recommend the best (no system change).
cmd.apply.help: Apply recommended DNS + NRPT split (admin required).
cmd.restore.help: Restore from the latest backup (admin required).
cmd.force-doh.help: Auto-pick best DoH from top 5 by speed + cleanliness; bypasses port 53 hijack.
msg.need_admin: This command requires administrator privileges. Open PowerShell as Administrator and try again.
msg.no_adapter: No active network adapter found. Check your connection.
msg.benchmarking: Benchmarking each DoH provider via real HTTPS queries (15-40s)…
msg.no_doh_passed: No DoH provider passed (all timed out or polluted). Check connectivity or use --intl-dns/--cn-dns.
msg.doh_registered: Registered {n} DoH templates (HTTPS:443, router can't intercept)
msg.dns_set: Set adapter DNS to {ips}, Windows auto-upgrades to DoH
msg.nrpt_added: Added {n} NRPT split rules (Chinese domains -> CN DoH)
msg.hijack_detected: Almost every overseas DNS responded < 5ms — physically impossible. Your network (most likely a soft router) is intercepting/caching DNS at UDP port 53.
msg.verify_steps:
Verify:
  1. Open https://1.1.1.1/help — should say "Using DNS over HTTPS (DoH): Yes"
  2. Re-test QQ login / Google

Restore: python run.py restore
msg.press_enter: Press Enter to return to the menu…
msg.hosts_analysed: hosts analysed: {total} lines, {active} active entries, {issues} issue(s)
msg.hosts_clean: hosts file looks clean — no repair needed.
msg.outage_all_ok: All five layers reachable — no outage detected.
msg.hijack_yes: Local network IS intercepting DNS at port 53.
msg.hijack_no: No port-53 hijack detected — DNS path looks legitimate.
msg.hijack_unknown: Could not reach any overseas DNS — can't tell yet.
menu.lang.name: Switch language
menu.lang.short: Re-pick the UI language at any time.
menu.lang.long: Open the language picker. The choice is persisted to .netmedic_config.json so subsequent runs reuse it.
menu.country.name: Switch country / region
menu.country.short: Tell NetMedic where you are so the right DNS pool is preferred.
menu.country.long: Choose your country or region. NetMedic will rank DNS providers based on which ones tend to perform best from there. AUTO uses your IP-based location.
menu.check.name: Full network checkup
menu.check.short: Adapter, ping, DNS speed, pollution, MTU — everything in one go.
menu.check.long: Inspect the active adapter, ping domestic + foreign hosts, benchmark public DNS speed, run the GFW poison-IP check, and report MTU. Read-only — does not modify anything.
menu.recommend.name: Recommend best DNS
menu.recommend.short: Speed + pollution test, suggest a config (does not change the system).
menu.recommend.long: Run benchmarks and pollution detection, then print a recommendation: which Chinese DNS, which foreign DNS, and which split-DNS namespaces. Detects port-53 hijacks and warns instead of silently picking garbage.
menu.apply.name: Apply (UDP/53)
menu.apply.short: Write the recommended DNS + NRPT rules to the active adapter.
menu.apply.long: Set the adapter DNS to the fastest clean foreign DNS, then write NRPT rules so Chinese namespaces resolve via the fastest Chinese DNS. Original state is backed up to backups/latest.json so restore is one command away.
menu.restore.name: Restore from backup
menu.restore.short: Roll the adapter DNS + NRPT rules back to the previous state.
menu.restore.long: Read backups/latest.json and put back what was there before. Removes any NRPT rules NetMedic added (identified by the [NetMedic] comment tag).
menu.force_doh.name: Force DoH (bypass router hijack)
menu.force_doh.short: Switch to encrypted DNS over HTTPS:443 — soft router can't intercept.
menu.force_doh.long: Benchmark every DoH provider over real HTTPS, run pollution detection, then show a Top-5 menu. The pick becomes the primary DoH; Chinese namespaces are pinned to a Chinese DoH via NRPT. Requires Windows 11 (uses native DoH support).
menu.bench_doh.name: Benchmark DoH providers
menu.bench_doh.short: Just measure DoH latency + cleanliness, no system change.
menu.bench_doh.long: Send real HTTPS DoH queries to every supported provider, time them, and probe each one for pollution. Useful before deciding which to apply.
menu.flush.name: Flush DNS cache
menu.flush.long: Clears the Windows DNS resolver cache. Useful after editing hosts, switching DNS, or chasing a stale-record bug.
menu.status.name: Show status
menu.status.short: Display the current adapter and NRPT rules.
menu.status.long: Read-only summary of the active network adapter (alias, IP, gateway, current DNS) plus all NRPT name-resolution policies, including those NetMedic owns.
menu.hosts_fix.name: Repair hosts file
menu.hosts_fix.short: Find and remove duplicate / blackhole / malformed hosts entries.
menu.hosts_fix.long: Analyse C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts: detect duplicate hostnames, blackhole entries pointing to 0.0.0.0/127.0.0.1 (typical ad-blocker leftovers), and malformed lines. Optionally rewrite the file with issues fixed; never touches lines inside another tool's marker block.
menu.outage.name: Diagnose network outage
menu.outage.short: Step through loopback → gateway → Internet → DNS → HTTPS to locate where it broke.
menu.outage.long: Five-layer probe that stops at the first failure and points you at the right fix. Tells loopback failure apart from gateway failure apart from DNS failure apart from HTTPS failure — so you don't randomly toggle settings.
menu.hijack.name: Detect router DNS hijack
menu.hijack.short: Tell whether your gateway is intercepting / caching port 53 traffic.
menu.hijack.long: Sends UDP probes to several reputable overseas DNS and times them. If most respond in <5 ms, that's physically impossible from any China-side network — the router is intercepting port 53 and impersonating the upstream. Suggests Force-DoH as the fix.
menu.exit.name: Exit
menu.exit.long: Close the launcher. Settings are already persisted.
scope.country.name: Country only
scope.country.short: Only DoH providers based in your country / region.
scope.country_plus.name: Country + global majors
scope.all.name: All providers
